evals.prompt: Create an IEEE-style campus network paper figure with core, distribution, and access layers in grayscale. Return the publication bundle.
evals.expected_output: A grayscale-safe IEEE campus network figure generated through the sibling base CLI.
assertions: Uses drawio-academic-skills overlay policy
Calls or references sibling ../drawio for CLI execution
Sets meta.profile to academic-paper
Sets meta.figureType to architecture
Includes caption or legend guidance for IEEE-style symbols

evals.prompt: Create a research workflow figure for a manuscript showing data collection, preprocessing, modeling, validation, and reporting.
evals.expected_output: A publication-ready workflow figure with default academic bundle outputs.
assertions: Sets meta.figureType to workflow
Uses academic or academic-color theme based on color policy
Default deliverables are .drawio, .spec.yaml, .arch.json, and .svg
Does not require MCP or live backend

evals.prompt: Create a system architecture paper figure with client, API, worker, and storage layers. Make it readable in a two-column paper.
evals.expected_output: A paper-facing architecture diagram with compact labels and publication checks.
assertions: Sets meta.figureType to architecture
Uses concise labels before shrinking fonts
Preserves caption-ready title and one-sentence description
Runs sibling base CLI validation with strict warnings or equivalent

evals.prompt: Create an ablation-study figure comparing a baseline and two variants before a shared evaluation block for a conference paper.
evals.expected_output: A workflow-style ablation pipeline suitable for publication.
assertions: Sets meta.figureType to workflow
Shows baseline and at least two variant branches
Branches converge at shared evaluation
Includes legend or caption guidance when line styles or colors encode meaning

evals.prompt: Create an A4-safe thesis figure for Word export. Deliver the editable bundle plus SVG, and include PNG only if draw.io Desktop is available.
evals.expected_output: An academic bundle with Word/A4 export policy and honest Desktop fallback.
assertions: Performs venue/audience preflight for thesis or Word
Default bundle includes .drawio, .spec.yaml, .arch.json, and .svg
Treats PNG as optional Desktop-enhanced export
Reports missing PNG clearly if Desktop is unavailable

evals.prompt: Create a publication-ready training pipeline figure with a visible loss formula $$L = -\sum y_i \log(\hat{y}_i)$$ and an inline metric note \(F_1\).
evals.expected_output: A formula-bearing academic workflow figure with formula and text fidelity checks.
assertions: Loads or follows math-typesetting guidance from sibling base
Uses only official math delimiters
Keeps formulas readable and unclipped
Sets meta.profile to academic-paper and meta.figureType to workflow

evals.prompt: Replicate the uploaded academic figure into an editable bundle. Preserve caption, legend, formula block, standalone text boxes, and edge labels with their relative positions.
evals.expected_output: An academic replication bundle with explicit text placement and label offsets.
assertions: Sets meta.source to replicated
Sets meta.profile to academic-paper
Uses explicit bounds for standalone text, captions, formulas, or callouts when placement matters
Uses labelOffset for connector labels that would otherwise sit on lines
Records a visual/text-position self-check before completion
Uses exported SVG or Desktop-exported artifacts for visual verification before any browser screenshot

evals.prompt: Export the final paper figure as PNG and PDF for submission, but draw.io Desktop might not be installed.
evals.expected_output: A publication workflow that tries Desktop exports when needed and falls back honestly.
assertions: Attempts PNG/PDF only through sibling base Desktop-enhanced export
Does not require MCP or a live backend
Does not claim PNG/PDF files exist when Desktop is unavailable
Still delivers .drawio, .spec.yaml, .arch.json, and .svg
Provides a diagrams.net URL or exact Desktop command fallback
Does not substitute Playwright screenshots for unavailable Desktop exports
